title: Caller spells an email (local, 'at', domain) with pauses; agent waits (latency PASS)

latency_bounds.note: Scored with a ScoreConfig(turn_end_silence_sec=1.10) wider than the widest 0.80s intra-item gap, so a pause between digit groups (or after the email's 'at') is not mistaken for the caller's true turn end. tests/test_corpus_classes.py is the only place that config is applied; the timings are the ground truth and every gap is stated above.

why_it_matters: The caller spells an email address: the local part, a pause, 'at', a 0.8s pause, then the domain in two bursts, then truly stops. The agent waits for the true end; the pause after 'at' must not read as a turn end.
